commit.message:
feat: BullMQ job queues para processamento assíncrono de mensagens

Webhook WhatsApp agora valida e enfileira em <100ms.
Workers BullMQ processam Gemini AI, envio e logging em paralelo.
- 3 filas: whatsapp-message, n8n-message, dead-letter
- message-processor.js: lógica extraída dos webhooks
- worker.js: entry point separado com Socket.IO Redis emitter
- Bull Board dashboard em /admin/queues
- Deduplicação por jobId, retry 3x com backoff exponencial
- docker-compose: serviço worker com replicas configurável
- Redis com persistência AOF + maxmemory 2gb
- n8n mantém processamento síncrono (espera resposta HTTP)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stress double-counting in CropModel and add AquaCrop yield comparison

engine/app/models/crop_model.py:
- update_daily(): remove overall_stress_factor from GDD accumulation.
  GDD is thermal time (temperature only); stress never changes air temp.
  Stress is applied exactly once — to daily biomass via RUE (line 156).
  Before fix: N-stress 0.31 reduced GDD clock by 69%, crop stuck at V2
  after 120 d (333 GDD). After fix: crop reaches R3 in 120 d (1,564 GDD).
- get_final_yield(): apply harvest_index (DSSAT/AquaCrop approach).
  Previously ignored self.harvest_index entirely and returned raw
  reproductive_biomass. Now: reproductive_biomass * HI if flowered,
  else total_biomass * HI as fallback. Resolves dead-parameter bug.

compare_yields.py (new):
- Standalone script comparing AgroVisus vs AquaCrop-OSPy for a 120-day
  IL corn simulation (May 1, SandyLoam, Champaign climate data).
- AgroVisus: corn template, 180 kg N/ha, irrigated — yields 6,223 kg/ha
  (within 5,500–8,500 target range, 31% below AquaCrop upper bound).
- AquaCrop: rainfed at FC, no disease — yields 8,992 kg/ha at day 120,
  10,750 kg/ha at full maturity (day 131).

All 110 pytest tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 15 audit fixes - streak overhaul, scanner validation, store and UI fixes

Streak system:
- Freeze only covers exactly 1 missed day (not multi-day gaps)
- Milestone dates now tracked (was always undefined)
- Removed unreachable code in recordScan
- scannedToday persisted across app restarts
- Streak re-checks when app returns to foreground

Scanner:
- Barcode validation now used on manual entry
- OCR response validated before returning success
- Ingredient matching uses word boundaries (no more false positives)

Stores & UI:
- Profile canCreateMore uses proper Zustand selector
- Subscription month reset uses ISO strings and DRY helper
- User store auto-initializes profile on mutations
- Paywall: fixed package selection race condition, friendly error messages
- Compare store returns replacement info with haptic feedback
- AI explanation and OCR fetch have 30s timeout
- Shopping list refresh timeout no longer stacks

commit.message:
Add C forward declarations for all JS FFI imports

Clang emits implicit function declaration errors for all mac# JS FFI
functions since they are WASM imports with no C prototype. Add %{} blocks
with extern forward declarations in each module so clang compiles cleanly.

Fixes #32

commit.message:
Use enhanced grayscale tiles for Claude + wider crop margins + smarter bottom detection

- Use enhanced grayscale (not B&W) for Claude Vision tiles: B&W thresholding
  destroys character anti-aliasing that vision models use to distinguish
  similar chars (0/O, 1/I, S/5, B/8). Keep B&W only for Tesseract OCR.
- Contrast 2x + Sharpness 3x on grayscale crop before tiling
- Crop X margins: 60→200px on each side to capture edge columns (QTY)
  that may not have been detected by header OCR
- Bottom detection: require ALL three scan columns (L/C/R) to agree on
  a white gap, min_gap 60→120px to ignore intra-table row separators
  and only trigger on true end-of-table whitespace
- Rotate grayscale image alongside B&W during rotation correction
- Clean up img_gray in loop memory management

commit.message:
Fix torch.load PyTorch 2.6 compatibility across codebase

Add weights_only=False to all torch.load calls that load steering
objects, weight modifications, and other serialized data containing
numpy scalars. PyTorch 2.6 changed the default to weights_only=True,
causing crashes when loading files with numpy.core.multiarray.scalar.

Every `rescue OptionParser::ParseError` block now prints the command's
usage banner alongside the error message. Previously 14 of 16 rescue
blocks printed only the raw error (e.g. "invalid option: --message")
with no indication of valid options or recovery path.

Born from scar: an agent ran `carson deliver --message "..."` and got
back only "invalid option: --message" — neither the agent nor the user
understood what went wrong or what to do instead.

- Drift bias cappato 0.60 → 0.10 (impedisce ai segnali microstructure di dominare)

- Slippage check adattato per Kelly stake
- Test suite: 46 test (unit + backtest reale Binance + Polymarket CLOB)

commit.message:
Add IMOS/AODN wave buoy observation integration for marine page

Marine sites now display real wave buoy observations overlaid on wave
height and wave period forecast charts. Data is sourced from the AODN
cloud-optimised S3 bucket (Parquet format, public, no auth needed)
covering ~78 Australian buoy stations with hourly updates.

New buoy_service.py follows the obs_service.py pattern: haversine
station matching (200 km radius), in-memory cache with 30-min TTL,
thread-safe locking, and graceful degradation on network failure.

Changes:
- constants: add IMOS_WAVE_BUOYS registry (9 WA/NT buoy stations),
  matching/cache/styling constants
- buoy_service: new service for S3 Parquet fetch, caching, matching
- marine_charts: add _add_buoy_obs_trace() helper (cyan, triangle
  markers), update create_wave_chart/create_wave_period_chart
- marine page: wire buoy data into master callback, add buoy status
  badge (cyan), add buoy stat card with latest Hs/Tp
- tests: 30 new tests (buoy constants + buoy service) — 181 total pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: support raw_confidence field from ADR-0016 calibrated scoring

When calibration is enabled, the backend returns both confidence_score
(calibrated) and raw_confidence (uncalibrated). The ConfidencePill now
shows "Raw → Calibrated" when the two values differ, and the REFUSED
explanation includes both scores. Backward compatible — raw_confidence
defaults to null and the UI renders identically when absent or equal.

- Add raw_confidence to QueryResponse type
- Update ConfidencePill to accept rawScore prop
- Update REFUSED explanation with calibrated wording
- Add query-ok-calibrated.json fixture
- Add contract test for calibrated scoring (26 tests total)

commit.message:
feat: support multi-page/multi-image supplier invoice import

Invoices often span multiple pages (multi-page PDFs or multiple photos).
Previously only the first page was processed, silently dropping line items
from subsequent pages.

Backend:
- Vision API accepts multiple images in a single call, extracting all line
  items across all pages into one consolidated result
- PDF renderer now processes ALL pages (capped at 20) instead of just page 1
- Upload endpoint accepts multiple files, stores each as a separate attachment
- Worker fetches all attachments for an invoice instead of just one

Frontend:
- Upload dropzone accepts multiple files with drag & drop
- Shows file list with individual sizes and remove buttons
- Sends all files via FormData to the updated endpoint

commit.message:
feat(s5): payout monitor — stuck detection job (STA-156) (#161)

* feat(s5): payout monitor — stuck detection job (STA-156)

Add scheduled job that detects stuck payouts (PAYOUT_INITIATED or
PAYOUT_PROCESSING older than 2 hours) and escalates them to
PAYOUT_FAILED → MANUAL_REVIEW with FiatPayoutFailed event publishing.

- PayoutMonitorJob: @Scheduled every 5min, @ConditionalOnProperty toggle
- PayoutMonitorCommandHandler: per-order TX isolation (REQUIRES_NEW)
- PayoutMonitorConfig: @ConfigurationProperties for threshold/interval
- PayoutMonitorProperties: domain port for config
- ClockConfig: standalone Clock bean (always available)
- 6 unit tests covering stuck initiated/processing, recent skip, empty

Closes STA-156

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(s5): address CodeRabbit review — TOCTOU guard, batch resilience, cached TX template (STA-156)

- Re-fetch order inside REQUIRES_NEW transaction to guard against TOCTOU
  races (e.g. webhook settling a payout between query and escalation)
- Skip escalation if order status changed since initial query
- Wrap per-order escalation in try-catch so one failure doesn't abort batch
- Cache TransactionTemplate as field instead of creating per invocation
- Add TOCTOU guard test (order already completed → skip)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
